summary.name: High-Conviction Mega-Cap Rotation vs QQQ
summary.author: Zheng Tian
summary.description: The strategy is a benchmark relative equity rotation designed to maximize information ratio versus QQQ by only taking idiosyncratic risk when there is high conviction that a single mega cap stock will outperform the Nasdaq 100 over the next week. It trades a fixed universe of large cap US technology equities (AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA, AMD) and otherwise holds QQQ to suppress tracking error during low signal regimes. The alpha thesis is that short horizon relative outperformance
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a benchmark relative equity rotation designed to maximize information ratio versus QQQ by only taking idiosyncratic risk when there is high conviction that a single mega cap stock will outperform the Nasdaq 100 over the next week. It trades a fixed universe of large cap US technology equities (AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA, AMD) and otherwise holds QQQ to suppress tracking error during low signal regimes. The alpha thesis is that short horizon relative outperformance can be predicted from recent 10 day momentum and contemporaneous active return (stock 10 day return minus QQQ 10 day return), capturing time varying leadership and rotation within the tech complex while explicitly framing the signal in active return space.

Model training occurs monthly using approximately 500 daily observations and labels each stock as a winner if it beats QQQ over the subsequent 5 trading days. Daily, the strategy scores each stock and requires a high probability threshold (greater than 0.70) to act; if triggered, it fully concentrates into the single highest scored stock, otherwise it remains in QQQ. Position sizing is essentially all in (about 98 percent of capital) with a full liquidation before switching to avoid margin usage; risk control is primarily via the high confidence filter and the explicit fallback to the benchmark when forecasts are uncertain.
